zh.Cork, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville於1335年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville33歲，母親Agnes de Chanteloup35歲。他和Marie DE LA MARCHE至少有1個兒子。他去世於1400年5月21日在義大利享年65歲。

zh-hans.Cork, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville于1335年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville33岁，母亲Agnes de Chanteloup35岁。他和Marie DE LA MARCHE至少有1个儿子。他去世于1400年5月21日在意大利享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Paynel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Payne
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville was born in 1335, in Normandy, France, his father, Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville, was 33 and his mother, Agnes de Chanteloup, was 35. He had at least 1 son with Marie DE LA MARCHE. He died on 21 May 1400, in Italy, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville est né en 1335, à Normandie, France, son père, Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Chanteloup, avait 35 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Marie DE LA MARCHE. Il est décédé le 21 mai 1400, à Italie, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville 1335 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville 33 und seine Mutter Agnes de Chanteloup 35. Er hatte mit Marie DE LA MARCHE mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 21. Mai 1400 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Italien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville è nato il nel 1335 nel Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Chanteloup, aveva 35 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Marie DE LA MARCHE. Morì il 21 maggio 1400, nel Italia, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricquevilleが1335年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricquevilleは33歳，母Agnes de Chanteloupは35歳であった。彼はMarie DE LA MARCHEとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1400年5月21日にイタリアで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville(이)가 1335년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Chanteloup(은)는 35세였다. 그는 Marie DE LA MARCHE(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1400년 5월 21일 이탈리아에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville nasceu em 1335, em Normandie, França, seu pai, Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Chanteloup, tinha 35 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Marie DE LA MARCHE. Ele faleceu em 21 de maio de 1400, em Itália, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville родился в 1335 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Chanteloup, 35. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Marie DE LA MARCHE. Он умер 21 мая 1400 г. в местности Италия в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Paynel - Heer van Bricqueville nació en 1335, en Normandie, Francia, su papá, Foulques V Paynel - Baron de Hambye, Seigneur de Bricqueville, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Agnes de Chanteloup, tenía 35 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Marie DE LA MARCHE. Murió el 21 de mayo de 1400, en Italia, a la edad de 65 años.
